apply: Calculate the mean, median, and mode of a given set of data using formulas.
Calculate the standard deviation and variance of a given set of data.
Apply the concept of skewness to a real-life scenario.
Use the concept of interquartile range to interpret a given set of data.
Calculate the coefficient of variation for a given set of data.
analyze: Analyze the distribution of a given set of data using measures of central tendency.
Compare the mean, median, and mode of two or more datasets.
Interpret the results of a statistical analysis using measures of central tendency.
Identify the strengths and limitations of different measures of central tendency.
Apply the concept of standard deviation to identify patterns in a given set of data.
remember: Recall the definitions of mean, median, and mode.
Explain the concept of mean deviation and range.
List the types of averages (arithmetic mean, geometric mean, harmonic mean).
Define and explain the concept of standard deviation.
Calculate the mean, median, and mode of a given set of data.
understand: Distinguish between the different types of averages and their uses.
Understand the concept of skewness and its types.
Explain the concept of interquartile range and its significance.
Understand the concept of standard error and its application.
Explain the concept of coefficient of variation and its uses.
